mnemonics: V for Volatile: Vfork's child uses parent's volatile space until exec/exit
keyPoints: The child process from vfork() runs in the parent's address space
The parent is suspended until the child calls _exit() or exec()
Modifying data in the child (except for the variable used with _exit) or performing library calls leads to undefined behavior
commonMistakes: Assuming the child has its own memory space like fork()
Having the child return from the function instead of using _exit()
Letting the child modify variables or call functions before exec/exit
